module_signals.finding: Evidence quality is high: all load-bearing facts trace to primary filings dated <= as-of -- the Q2'24 earnings release (8-K, Aug 1 2024) + Q2'24 10-Q, FY23 10-K, and the Apr-2-2024 Foundry-framework 8-K. As-of price verified ($21.48 close, -26% day, ~$92B equity). The binding uncertainties are FORWARD, not evidentiary: (a) whether the foundry break-even path (~2027) and 18A ramp are credible from outside, and (b) whether the H1 OCF-vs-capex gap is bridgeable without further dilution/leverage now that the dividend is suspended. Per harness, forward gaps become a SIZE HAIRCUT, not an automatic WATCH cap -- but here the financial reality (M4) is itself negative, so confidence being high does NOT rescue the case.

Theme is real (AI-compute demand + reshoring/foundry sovereignty), but the MECHANISM by which Intel captures it is weak and unproven as-of. Why now: a -26% one-day repricing on a Q2 miss + a sweeping reset (dividend suspension, >15% headcount cut, >20% capex cut). What the market may be OVER-weighting: the single-quarter optics and the dividend cut as pure distress. What it may be UNDER-weighting: ~$29B gross liquidity and a self-funded reset that extends runway. BUT the core mechanism is broken in the as-of window: data center/AI -- the largest live profit pool in semis -- is being captured by Nvidia (~90%+ accelerator share) and AMD (record DC quarter, >$1B MI300X), not Intel (DCAI rev -3% YoY); the foundry is pre-EUV (>85% of wafer volume) and loses ~$2.8B/quarter; and 'process leadership returns via 18A' is a management narrative, not a realized fact. This is a CONTESTED TURNAROUND inside a structurally challenged franchise -- the live theme exists but Intel is on the losing side of the bottleneck as-of. Net mildly negative.

The profit pools are moving AGAINST Intel. (1) Data center / AI -- the richest growth pool -- is captured by Nvidia/AMD; Intel's DCAI revenue fell YoY and the AI-accelerator wave is being missed in the as-of window. (2) Intel Foundry is a NEGATIVE profit pool: -$2.83B in Q2'24, -$7.0B FY23, >85% pre-EUV/uncompetitive-cost wafers, with break-even a management-claimed ~2027 (years out). (3) The one durable, profitable pool is CCG (PC): $7.4B rev +9%, $2.5B segment op income, AI-PC unit leadership -- but PC is a mature/secularly-pressured pool and is funding everything else. OPERATOR/team life-arc grade = ~3/5 (operator_background.md): exceptional founder-market fit (Gelsinger) + clean one-share-one-vote governance + independent chair, BUT high key-man concentration, short team cohesion (~2.5 yrs as a unit), evangelist-ahead-of-results pattern, and an unproven irreversible capital bet. A ~3/5 team neither raises nor lowers the durability ceiling -- it does NOT support a high-durability read. Net: economics captured by competitors + a money-losing manufacturing pool + a neutral-at-best operator grade = -1 (close to -2; held at -1 only because CCG remains a genuine, profitable, defensible pool).

Accounting REFUTES the cheap-turnaround optics; this is the binding module. Revenue only -1% YoY, but Q2'24 swung to a GAAP operating loss of -$1,964M (op margin -15.3%), non-GAAP operating margin collapsed to ~0.2%, and GAAP net loss attributable to Intel was -$1.6B; $943M restructuring. NORMALIZED/cycle economics are poor: ex-the lone CCG engine, the company barely breaks even at the segment line ($47M total segment op income) and the foundry drag is structural, not one-off. The decisive item is CASH: H1 2024 GAAP operating cash flow was just $1,069M against $11,652M gross capex -- a ~$10.6B gap bridged by ~$11.9B of partner contributions + debt issuance; the headline 'positive' six-month adjusted FCF ($8,155M) is financially engineered by those offsets, not generated by the business. Balance sheet: ~$29.3B cash+ST investments BUT ~$53.0B total debt, and the company suspended a decades-old dividend specifically to preserve liquidity for the fab buildout. Per harness trap-veto criteria, BOTH 'normalized earnings far below trailing' AND 'capex/debt/dilution absorb owner earnings' are satisfied. This is a -2 warning.

Falling-knife-vs-turnaround-bottom test. The STRUCTURAL-DECLINE reading is well-supported on as-of evidence: (1) share/position loss in the growth pool (DCAI down while Nvidia/AMD take AI compute); (2) a manufacturing business that loses ~$7B/yr with a multi-year, management-claimed path to break-even and >85% uncompetitive pre-EUV wafers; (3) ~$12B/yr+ gross capex funded by partner contributions + debt against ~$1B H1 operating cash flow -- capex absorbing owner earnings; (4) the dividend suspension itself signals cash strain, not strength. Classic value-trap setup the harness explicitly names ('meant to catch INTC...'); the trap to avoid is 'calling low P/E / sub-book a margin of safety.' DOES A HARD VETO (size->0) FIRE? Borderline. The harness hard-veto conditions (M3 profit-pool loss + M4 capex/debt absorbing owner earnings + M5 structural decline) are largely met. The one thing that STOPS a flat zero-REJECT veto: near-term SURVIVAL is not in question -- ~$29B gross liquidity plus the cost/capex cuts and the dividend suspension EXTEND runway; the balance sheet does not fail in the as-of window. So this is a STRONG risk warning that vetoes any STARTER-or-larger sizing and bars catching the knife at size, but stops just short of declaring imminent insolvency. -2, with the explicit note that it effectively caps the position rather than zeroing it outright.

At $21.48 (~$92B equity, ~26% one-day drop to the lowest level in over a decade) Intel is OPTICALLY cheap -- roughly at/around tangible book and a fraction of peak earnings power -- but optically cheap is exactly the trap signature for a structural_decline_trap. There is NO usable trailing/forward GAAP earnings multiple (the company is loss-making on a GAAP basis and guided to a Q3 GAAP loss of $(0.24)); 'normalized' earning power is unproven because it depends on a foundry that loses billions and a data-center franchise losing share. Price therefore does NOT create favorable asymmetry: the downside (continued foundry losses, further cash strain, possible dilution, no dividend) is not survivable-with-upside at a real position -- it is open-ended until the cash/foundry question resolves. Per the context gate, price is 'mostly irrelevant after veto' and low multiple is not MOS. The level is low enough to justify TRACKING the name (cheapness + a real, identifiable turnaround key variable), not to justify deploying real money. -1.

starter_zone: None at the as-of date. No STARTER is justified: a STARTER requires (harness §5) M5 to show no permanent impairment / balance-sheet death AND M6 to show survivable downside at small size -- here M4/M5 are -2 and the foundry cash gap is unresolved. Tracking sliver (0-0.5%) only, to force attention, NOT a bet.
add_zone: No add on price alone. Re-rate to a real STARTER (3-4%) ONLY on a forward CONFIRMATION that resolves the binding constraint: (a) Intel Foundry operating losses demonstrably past trough with a credible, externally-validated narrowing AND (b) operating cash flow rising to cover a materially larger share of capex WITHOUT partner-contribution/debt flattering AND (c) DCAI stabilizing rather than ceding more share. Price cheapness is not an add trigger.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Do NOT chase / do NOT average down on the falling knife. For an EXISTING holder: TRIM hard now (cut toward tracking) -- the position should not be sized to the unproven, irreversible, cash-absorbing fab bet. This is a methodology trigger #4 action (thesis of self-funding turnaround is not yet confirmed; capex absorbs owner earnings), not a 'price got low so hold' decision.
binding_constraint: The foundry cash-burn-vs-thin-operating-cash-flow gap. H1 2024 GAAP operating cash flow of only ~$1.07B against ~$11.65B gross capex -- bridged by ~$11.9B of partner contributions + debt against ~$53B total debt -- means the multi-year, largely irreversible fab buildout is being funded by financial engineering, not by the business, while the richest profit pool (AI/data-center compute) is captured by Nvidia/AMD. This is what holds the position at 0% / WATCH (a near-veto SIZE constraint): real money cannot be deployed until either the foundry losses are demonstrably past trough AND operating cash flow covers a materially larger share of capex, or the data-center franchise stabilizes. Cheapness (sub-book, ~$92B) is NOT a margin of safety here -- it is the trap signature.
kill_criteria: Foundry operating losses do NOT trough in 2024 as management claims (losses flat or widening into 2025) -- the central turnaround premise fails; stay out / cut further.

Operating cash flow stays far below gross capex and the gap continues to be bridged by partner contributions + new debt, OR Intel raises equity / cuts capex again under duress -> cash strain is structural, not transitional; reject.
DCAI revenue/share keeps eroding while Nvidia/AMD extend AI-compute and server-CPU lead -> the data-center profit pool is permanently lost, not cyclically soft.
CCG (the lone profit engine) weakens materially (AI-PC demand disappoints or PC share erodes) -> the cash cow funding the bet is impaired; exit.
18A milestones slip or the 'regain process leadership' narrative is contradicted by independent (non-management) evidence -> the manufacturing thesis is broken.

Total debt rises further / leverage metrics deteriorate / any credit-rating or covenant stress -> balance-sheet survival, not just returns, becomes the question -> this would convert the near-veto into a hard zero.
runner_dissent: Two-sided dissent; net I am comfortable with WATCH/0%, but I flag both edges. (1) Possible-too-harsh edge: this is a self-FUNDED reset (dividend suspension + capex/opex cuts ADD liquidity) at a once-dominant franchise with ~$29B gross cash and a still-profitable PC engine -- a disciplined value investor (Duan-style) could argue for a tiny TRACKING sliver (~0.5%) to force attention rather than a flat REJECT, since near-term survival is not the issue and there IS a clean, identifiable key variable (foundry trough + cash coverage). I have set max_size 1% to permit exactly that tracking sliver. (2) Possible-too-soft edge: a stricter read fires a flat hard VETO -> REJECT, because the harness names INTC as the canonical structural_decline_trap and BOTH trap-veto conditions (normalized << trailing; capex/debt absorb owner earnings) are met -- the only reason I did not zero it to REJECT is that the balance sheet does not fail in the near term. I land on WATCH (not REJECT) because METHODOLOGY/harness say a structural-decline diagnosis WITH a named monitor and survivable near-term balance sheet is a tracking posture, not necessarily an outright avoid -- and uncertainty should be a haircut, here taken all the way to 0% real money. The discipline: NO new money and TRIM an existing position now; re-rate to a real STARTER only on a forward confirmation that the foundry is past trough and cash covers capex. I explicitly did NOT use any post-as-of information (no later price, no leadership change, no 18A/foundry outcome, no CHIPS stake).
